title: Declining short-term emission control opportunity for major events in Chinese cities

data_scope.neighboring_cities: defined per event in article/Supplementary (no single aggregate count)
data_scope.outputs: RETOMI2 retrieval + a-posteriori NOx emissions for 11 events

core_conclusion: Short-term emission controls staged around major events in Chinese cities are yielding shrinking returns. Across 11 events in east China (2010-2023), satellite NOx inversions show controls can be more effective in neighboring than host cities for some events, and the dominant abated sector has shifted from power to industry and transportation, indicating the cheap emission budget is already exhausted by long-term policy and that future controls must be more spatially and sectorally targeted.
why_it_matters: Provides the first rigorous, sector-resolved audit of widely-used 'blue-sky for the summit' controls, documents diminishing returns over time, reframes control from city-bounded to regional, and separates meteorology from genuine emission cuts.
why_nature: City-scale policy with a transferable evaluation method; couples human/industrial activity with satellite-observed emission fields; directly policy-translatable for sector and city targeting; releases an improved NO2 product (RETOMI2) and open ML+inversion pipeline as infrastructure.
added_value_to_my_research: SHAP-attributed emission inversion -> explainable spatial-prediction template transferable to accessibility models
Satellite + mobility fusion -> near-real-time hybrid emission tracker and exposure backbone
Population-weighted NOx plume -> environmental-justice 2SFCA with clean air as supply
Physics-informed spatiotemporal graph with ERA5 advection -> reusable diffusion-aware GeoAI operator
Marginal-abatement curves -> efficiency-vs-equity allocation optimizer reused from EV-charging siting
